definition: Physical/digital infrastructure delivery, access expansion, project implementation, and infrastructure reliability evidence.
Contribution to economic output, revenue, market development, productivity, and measurable sector growth.
Digital skills, jobs, training, enterprise support, and youth-focused capacity-building outcomes.
Measured continuity, responsiveness, risk reduction, and service delivery during shocks or crises.
Evidence quality, public reporting, auditability, open records, consumer protection, and verifiable accountability.
Management of regulated public utility or essential-service institutions and measurable user outcomes.
Policy, technology, institutional process, or service-design innovation backed by public evidence.
